title: cleomella longipes
intro: Cleomella longipes, also known as the Mexican poppy or poppy cleome, is a flowering plant species in the genus Cleomella, characterized by its showy, papery bracts and delicate white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Cleomella longipes include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems, making it a valuable remedy in herbal medicine. Cleomella longipes can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for a range of herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
